body: The Donald Trump 2.0 administration seeks to revive its tariff policies that were struck down by a US Supreme Court ruling.

title: JCI Opens Slightly Higher but Caution Dominates Ahead of Idul Fitri Holiday
body: JCI opened 0.12% higher at 7,398 but caution prevailed as geopolitical tensions and the Idul Fitri holiday kept investors defensive.

title: Indonesia Says Iran War Hurts National Economy in Three Ways
body: Finance Minister says Iran war could hurt Indonesia via higher oil imports, capital outflows and rising fiscal pressure.

title: How Iran War and Surging Oil Prices Are Affecting US Consumers at Gas Pump and Beyond
body: If oil prices remain elevated, fresh foods that must be transported quickly could see price hikes more quickly than packaged foods.

title: Indonesia Customs Revenue Falls 14.7% as Tax Collection Surges 30%
body: Tax revenue increased by about Rp 57 trillion compared with January–February 2025, helping the government accelerate spending programs.

title: OJK Sets $1.5 Trillion Stock Market Goal for 2031
body: OJK targets Rp 25 quadrillion ($1.5 trillion) in market capitalization by 2031, alongside reforms to strengthen market integrity.

title: New OJK Chair Friderica Pledges Stronger Oversight and Investor Protection
body: The House confirms Friderica as chair of the Financial Services Authority, pledging stronger oversight and consumer protection.

title: Indonesia Disburses $3.3 Billion Ramadan Bonus for State Employees
body: Indonesia distributes Rp 55 trillion in Ramadan bonuses to over 10 million civil servants, soldiers, police and retirees ahead of Eid.

title: B50 or B60: Stagnant Palm Oil Output Can Hamper Indonesia’s Biodiesel Dream
body: Gapki warns the impact of diverting exports for local supply as this can cause the biodiesel program to lose its source of funding.

title: Prabowo Targets $50 Billion Annual Contribution From Danantara
body: President Prabowo sets $50 billion annual contribution target for sovereign wealth fund Danantara from a minimum 5% return on assets.

title: Finance Minister Dismisses 'Overheating' Concerns as Inflation Hits 4.76%
body: Purbaya says the uptick in inflation data does not accurately reflect underlying inflationary pressures.

title: Rupiah Hits Record Low Beyond 1998 Crisis, Still Outperforms Regional Peers: Minister
body: The rupiah hit a record low beyond the 1998 crisis level, though Indonesia’s finance minister says its decline remains milder than peers.

title: GoTo Narrows Net Loss by 77% in 2025 as Revenue Climbs
body: GoTo cut its net loss by 77% in 2025 as revenue rose 15% and costs stayed tight.

title: Indonesia Stocks Slide as Oil Rebounds, Global Tensions Pressure Risk Appetite
body: JCI fell 0.69% to 7,389 as rising oil prices and Iran war tensions weighed on sentiment despite mixed performance in Asian markets.

title: Iran War Causes Indonesian Palm Oil Export Costs to Soar 50%
body: Countries have no choice but to buy Indonesian palm oil amid the Iran war as the vegetable crop is a "daily necessity", Gapki says.

title: Indonesia Startup Funding Falls to $355.7M in 2025 Amid Global Tightening
body: Indonesia startup funding fell to $355.7M in 2025, reflecting tighter global capital and a shift toward more disciplined investment.

title: Antam Gold Jumps Rp 40,000 per Gram on Wednesday
body: Antam gold surged to Rp 3.087 million per gram Wednesday, extending a 24% rally in 2026 as global uncertainty fuels safe-haven demand.

title: JCI Rises 0.58% at Open as Oil Retreats and Ramadan Consumption Outlook Holds
body: JCI advances to 7,484 as oil eases from recent spikes while investors watch global conflict risks and domestic demand.

title: Finance Minister Drops Plan to Dissolve Customs Directorate After Reform Progress
body: Purbaya drops plan to dissolve the Customs Directorate after reforms and improved performance earn praise from President Prabowo.

title: Finance Minister Swears in 1,500 Officials, Issues Corruption Warning
body: Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa warns officials they are not above the law as he swears in 1,500 staff amid tax and customs reforms.
